description:
Every JIT-emitted function (uniform and native, including typer specs) carries
two pieces of dead emission in its CLIF:

1. **Stray `v2 = iconst.i64 0` at the top of every entry block** — never
   referenced; persists in both uniform and native ABI fns.

2. **A dead `block1` halt epilogue** with the shape:

       block1:
           v_n = iconst.i64 2     ; tagged atom 0 (TAG_ATOM = 0b010)
           call fz_halt(host_ctx, v_n)
           return v_n             ; returns 2 from a (i64,i64)->i64 tail fn

   The block is unreachable (no predecessor), but Cranelift retains it through
   codegen.

   Suspicious side-finding: in `k_2_s4` (typed continuation spec) the block1's
   `call fn0(v1, v9)` passes 2 args even though that fn0's declared signature
   is `(i64) system_v` (1 arg). The Cranelift verifier evidently doesn't reject
   signature-mismatched calls in unreachable blocks. Confirm whether this is
   benign (DCE drops it pre-codegen) or whether the malformed call survives in
   machine code.

Reproduce:

    cargo run --release -- dump --emit clif --fn add1 fixtures/add1.fz

## Up-front investigation (do before filing implementation atoms)

- [ ] Locate the emission site. Candidates: `compile_fn` (src/ir_codegen.rs
      around line 2018-2070), `emit_return` (~2528), or a FunctionBuilder
      default. Confirm whether the iconst-0 and the halt block are produced by
      the same path or two unrelated ones.
- [ ] Determine whether the block1 halt is a vestige of an earlier
      trampoline/halt design (pre-.27/.29) or whether some current code path
      genuinely creates it as a fallback that DCE always prunes in practice.
- [ ] Confirm via objdump/disassembly whether the dead block survives into
      machine code or Cranelift's later passes drop it. If it's purely a CLIF
      artifact, the cleanup is cosmetic; if it's in the binary, this is a
      size/icache concern.
- [ ] Decide scope: pure CLIF hygiene (delete the emission) vs. a real
      correctness gap exposed by the k_2_s4 sig mismatch.

## Acceptance

- All four target fns in `fz dump --emit clif fixtures/add1.fz` lose the
  `v2 = iconst.i64 0` and the trailing `block1`.
- No regressions in any fixture (`expect_clif_contains` / `expect_clif_excludes`
  still pass; new excludes added for `add1.fz`).
- Investigation findings recorded in this ticket before implementation atoms
  are filed (per CLAUDE.md: no hidden surprises).
title: Remove dead block1 halt epilogue and stray entry-block iconst from compiled fns
